_note: Seattle HQ data. Update events, transit, and logistics as match days approach.

venue.name: Lumen Field
venue.notes: Capacity expanded for World Cup. Bag policy: clear bags only, max 12"x6"x12".
venue.parkingNote: No public parking at venue. Use transit or remote parking with shuttle.

transit.recommended: Link Light Rail — Stadium Station (1 block from Lumen Field)
tips: Trains run every 8–12 min on match days. Arrive 90 min early.
Westlake, Capitol Hill, University District, and Sea-Tac all connect via 1 Line.
Last train after match: approximately 1:00 AM.
King County Metro adding express bus routes from Park & Ride lots.
transit.parking: WSDOT Lot B (SoDo) — $35/day shuttle. Reserve in advance.
transit.rideshare: Designated pickup/dropoff at 1st Ave S & Edgar Martinez Dr S.

fanZones.name: Seattle FIFA Fan Festival
fanZones.notes: Free entry. Live entertainment, food, giant screens. Official FIFA fan zone.

fanZones.name: SoDo Watch Zone
fanZones.notes: Outdoor screen. First-come basis. Walking distance to Lumen Field.

preGame.name: The Pyramid Alehouse
preGame.notes: Classic SoDo match-day spot. Gets packed 2+ hours before kickoff.

preGame.name: Pioneer Square bars
preGame.notes: Best pre-game neighborhood energy. Try Central Saloon or Elysian Bar.

preGame.name: Pike Place Market
preGame.notes: Good for earlier arrivals. Head to SoDo 90 min before kickoff.

cityEnergyForecast.crowdNote: Belgium vs Egypt — noon kickoff, Mo Salah in Seattle. Belgian and Egyptian fans mix citywide.
USA vs Australia — USMNT at home at Lumen Field. Largest American crowd possible. Historic.
Bosnia-Herzegovina vs Qatar — noon kickoff. European fans traveling, quieter than USA or Belgium matches.
Egypt vs Iran — 8pm PT night kickoff, geopolitically charged. Sold out. Maximum atmosphere.
Round of 32 — Group G winner returns to Lumen Field for elimination knockout.
Round of 16 — Last Seattle match ever at a World Cup. One of the final 16 teams on earth.

transitPain.label: Moderate
transitPain.note: Belgium vs Egypt — noon kickoff (12pm PT). Earlier timing helps transit. Still busy.
tips: Noon kickoff is actually manageable — no evening commute overlap
Belgian and Egyptian fans arrive early — bars fill from 10am
Post-match: midday means easier transit and rideshare

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: USA vs Australia — noon kickoff. USMNT at Lumen Field will draw the biggest American crowd of the tournament.
tips: USA match = maximum crowd energy. Stadium full hours early
American Outlaws fan section will create the loudest atmosphere Seattle has ever seen
Post-match: despite noon KO, city celebrates all afternoon — plan for a full day

transitPain.label: Low
transitPain.note: Bosnia-Herzegovina vs Qatar — noon kickoff. Smaller fan followings, easiest transit day of the group stage.
tips: Most manageable transit day of all 4 group stage matches
Good day to explore SoDo and Pioneer Square without extreme crowds
Bosnia fans traveling from Europe will add energy — watch for scarves and songs

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: Egypt vs Iran — 8pm PT night kickoff. Most intense atmosphere of the group stage. Geopolitically charged.
tips: Night kickoff means Friday-night energy plus soccer energy — city is electric
Bars will be packed from 5pm. Pioneer Square is the pre-game destination
Post-match: 10pm+ crowd, limited transit — stay near SoDo 30 min before boarding Link

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: Knockout round. Single-elimination. City will be at fever pitch.
tips: Plan your entire day around this match
Stay near SoDo or Pioneer Square all afternoon
Book your post-match ride or accommodation in advance — transit will be mobbed for 90+ min after final whistle

transitPain.label: Very High
transitPain.note: Final Seattle knockout match. One of the last World Cup games here — ever.
tips: Make this a full day event
Book SoDo/Pioneer Square table in advance
This is the last time Lumen Field hosts a World Cup match — treat it accordingly

shouldIGoScores.label: Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Belgium vs Egypt — Mo Salah at Lumen Field. Could be one of the most dramatic matches of the group stage. Noon kickoff is manageable.

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: USA vs Australia at Lumen Field — USMNT on home soil in your city. The biggest American soccer moment in decades. If you're a Seattle fan, this is the match.

shouldIGoScores.label: Consider
shouldIGoScores.summary: Bosnia-Herzegovina vs Qatar — Bosnia's historic first WC game in Seattle. Less high-profile than USA or Egypt/Belgium matches. Best ticket value of the group stage.

shouldIGoScores.label: Strongly Go
shouldIGoScores.summary: Egypt vs Iran — night kickoff, geopolitical electricity, Group G stakes. The most atmospheric match of the four group stage games. Friday night at Lumen Field.

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: Knockout soccer at Lumen Field. Elimination match. Go.

shouldIGoScores.label: Don't miss it
shouldIGoScores.summary: The last World Cup match Seattle will ever host. Nothing close to this again.
